ddls:
CREATE TABLE "network_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric entry */,
  "round" INTEGER /* Round number of the testing */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the testing in seconds */,
  "packets_per_ms" INTEGER /* Packets sent per millisecond */,
  "receivers" INTEGER /* Number of receivers in the network */,
  "senders" INTEGER /* Number of senders in the network */,
  "rate_r" REAL /* Receiver rate in packets per second */,
  "rate_s" REAL /* Sender rate in packets per second */,
  "percent_sent" REAL /* Percentage of packets sent */,
  "percent_loss" REAL /* Percentage of packet loss */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the network segment */,
  "test_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the test was conducted */,
  "test_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the test */,
  "test_environment" TEXT /* Environment where the test was conducted */,
  "test_type" TEXT /* Type of test (e.g., stress test, regular monitoring) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_performance_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("segment_id") REFERENCES network_segments ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network segment */,
  "segment_name" TEXT /* Name of the network segment */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the network segment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the network segment */,
  "segment_type" TEXT /* Type of network segment (e.g., data center, remote office) */,
  "segment_status" TEXT /* Current status of the segment (e.g., active, maintenance) */,
  "segment_bandwidth" INTEGER /* Bandwidth capacity of the segment */,
  "segment_latency" REAL /* Average latency of the segment */,
  "segment_packet_loss" REAL /* Average packet loss of the segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., network engineer, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user for SMS notifications */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login time of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_notes" (
  "note_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each note */,
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance metric entry the note is associated with */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the note */,
  "note_text" TEXT /* Text content of the note */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the note was created */,
  "note_status" TEXT /* Status of the note (e.g., open, resolved) */,
  "last_updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the note was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("note_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_notes_performance_id FOREIGN KEY ("performance_id") REFERENCES network_performance ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_notes_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance metric entry that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., packet loss, rate increase) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the alert was created */,
  "alert_status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., active, resolved) */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "alert_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the alert in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_performance_id FOREIGN KEY ("performance_id") REFERENCES network_performance ("performance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each historical performance entry */,
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance metric entry */,
  "recorded_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the historical data was recorded */,
  "rate_r" REAL /* Receiver rate in packets per second */,
  "rate_s" REAL /* Sender rate in packets per second */,
  "percent_loss" REAL /* Percentage of packet loss */,
  "history_status" TEXT /* Status of the historical data entry (e.g., active, archived) */,
  "history_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or annotations for the historical data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_history_performance_id FOREIGN KEY ("performance_id") REFERENCES network_performance ("performance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_thresholds" (
  "threshold_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each threshold */,
  "metric" TEXT /* Metric name to which the threshold applies */,
  "value" REAL /* Threshold value */,
  "operator" TEXT /* Operator used to compare the metric value against the threshold (e.g., >, <, =) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the threshold */,
  "threshold_severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the threshold (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "threshold_action" TEXT /* Action to be taken when the threshold is triggered (e.g., send alert, log event) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("threshold_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alert_recipients" (
  "recipient_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each recipient */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who receives the alert */,
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* ID of the alert */,
  "notification_method" TEXT /* Method of notification (e.g., email, SMS) */,
  "recipient_status" TEXT /* Status of the recipient (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "last_notified" TEXT /* Date and time when the recipient was last notified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("recipient_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alert_recipients_alert_id FOREIGN KEY ("alert_id") REFERENCES alerts ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alert_recipients_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance metric entry the tag is associated with */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag */,
  "tag_status" TEXT /* Status of the tag (e.g., active, archived) */,
  "tag_description" TEXT /* Description of the tag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_tags_performance_id FOREIGN KEY ("performance_id") REFERENCES network_performance ("performance_id")
)
